error.signature: AI tells a traveler that Russia only issues visas through a slow embassy invitation process

verdict.summary: Since August 2023 Russia issues a unified electronic visa (e-visa) to citizens of around 55 countries for short tourism, business, or visits - applied for online a few days ahead, valid 60 days with a stay of up to 16 days. Other nationalities still need a consular visa with an invitation. Check current eligibility and travel advisories.

dead_ends.action: Assume every traveler to Russia needs a consular visa with an invitation letter
dead_ends.why_fails: Russia launched a unified e-visa in 2023 for around 55 eligible nationalities; for them the slow invitation-based consular process is no longer required for short stays.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The 2023 unified e-visa replaced the invitation process for eligible short-stay visitors.

dead_ends.action: Overstay the 16-day e-visa limit or use it for work/long stays
dead_ends.why_fails: The e-visa permits a stay of up to 16 days for tourism/business/visit only; working or overstaying breaches it and risks penalties.

workarounds.action: If eligible, apply for the unified e-visa online a few days before travel via the official MID portal.
workarounds.how: Use only the official electronic-visa.kdmid.ru site; apply at least 4 days ahead and carry the approved e-visa.

workarounds.action: If your nationality is not e-visa eligible, apply for a consular visa with the required invitation in advance.
workarounds.how: Arrange a consular visa and invitation if you are not on the e-visa list.
workarounds.tradeoff: Consular visas take longer and need an invitation.
